text:
Supported by
U.S. Vows to Block Iran’s Attempt to Shut Down Key Global Shipping Route
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that the disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz were “something we are dealing with.” And about 2,500 Marines were headed to the Middle East to bolster the war effort.
The leaders of America’s war effort vowed on Friday to thwart Iran’s attempts to block the Strait of Hormuz, calling Iran’s move on the crucial shipping route “desperation.” But they did not offer any timetable to restore safe navigation for the vast oil exports that had flowed through the channel.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said at a news conference that the disruptions in the strait were “something we are dealing with,” but added, “Don’t need to worry about it.”
title: U.S. Vows to Block Iran’s Attempt to Shut Down Strait of Hormuz
